product_overview: A React front-end to view and manage police warrants, allowing authenticated users to mark priority flags and update DP Region mapping with a prompted save flow. Authentication is handled via the /login route and main warrant interactions occur on the /warrants/:id detail page.
core_goals: Provide secure user authentication and session flow to access warrant data.
Expose a tactical warrant detail UI that allows marking priority flags and editing DP Region mapping.
Require explicit user confirmation when saving modifications and prevent accidental navigation away from unsaved changes.
Surface clear success and error states for save and authentication operations.

features.name: User Login
features.description: Authenticate users using email and password so they can access protected pages such as the dashboard and warrant details.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field -> Click Login button to submit -> See successful authentication -> Redirect to /dashboard -> See dashboard
Navigate to /login -> Fill email input field -> Fill password input field with incorrect password -> Click Login button to submit -> See authentication error message -> Remain on /login

features.name: Warrant Detail Edit
features.description: View and edit a warrant's details (priority flags and DP Region). Changes trigger a save prompt and must be confirmed to persist.
user_flows: Authenticate and navigate to /warrants/:id -> View warrant information -> Click priority control to mark as Priority -> Edit DP Region field -> Edit values -> Click save -> Trigger popup to save modifications -> Confirm save in popup -> See success confirmation message -> See updated warrant information on /warrants/:id

Authenticate and navigate to /warrants/:id -> View warrant information -> Edit DP Region field -> Attempt to navigate to /dashboard -> Trigger popup to save modifications -> Choose Cancel in popup -> Remain on /warrants/:id with unsaved changes visible

Authenticate and navigate to /warrants/:id -> View warrant information -> Edit values -> Click save -> Trigger popup to save modifications -> Confirm save in popup -> See save failure message (e.g. network/error) -> Keep edit form open on /warrants/:id for retry

features.name: User Login
features.description: Login with email and password credentials

features.name: Warrant Detail Edit
features.description: View and edit detail of police warrants, marking as priority, updating DP regions.
